H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong. Analyzers consensus: top-performing overstimulation protocol with strong user satisfaction. AutoGluon predicts 6.859 (overstim zone), slightly below observed mean. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, high music approval (9 'Loved it!'), and settle time ~10 minutes. YouTube views=30 represent highest reach in HEALING series though retention data unavailable.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Top choice for calming overwhelm - users consistently report feeling better and really liking the music, with decent discovery compared to other protocols.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong. Analyzers consensus: reliable overstimulation protocol with solid performance. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, varied settle times (5-20 minutes), and generally positive music feedback. YouTube views=5 indicate continued low discoverability. Performance consistent with H-OS-L1 but slightly lower mean score.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Reliable option for reducing overwhelm that works well for most users who find it.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Analyzers consensus: generally effective overstimulation protocol where subset reports incomplete settlement. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, settle time 5-10 minutes, but 7/23 reported 'did NOT settle'. YouTube views=8 reflect minimal reach. Suggests potential timing mismatches in entrainment curve for specific user subgroups.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Generally helpful for overwhelm, though some users report it doesn't fully settle them even after the full duration.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong. Analyzers consensus: strong efficacy for anxiety with high user satisfaction. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 (anxiety zone), slightly above observed mean. Qualitative data shows noticeable to significant improvements, median settle time ~10 minutes, and strong music approval. YouTube views=7 indicate minimal discoverability despite positive user reports.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: This anxiety protocol works well for most users, helping them feel significantly calmer. People really like the music and find it settles them in about 10 minutes.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Analyzers consensus: reliable anxiety protocol with moderate but consistent effects. Qualitative data shows mixed improvement levels, settle times ranging 5-20 minutes, and balanced rhythm perceptions. YouTube views=6 indicate very limited reach. Performance aligns with series expectations for descent anxiety protocols.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: A reliable choice for anxiety relief that delivers consistent, moderate improvements for most users who try it.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful. Analyzers consensus: standard anxiety protocol performing within expected parameters. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, settle time ~10 minutes, and generally positive music feedback. YouTube views=4 reflect minimal discoverability. No significant deviations from HEALING series anxiety protocol norms.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Standard anxiety protocol that helps users feel better, with most noticing improvements after about 10 minutes of listening.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.7, confidence=meaningful. Analyzers consensus: effective crisis protocol hampered by low discovery. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 (crisis zone), significantly above observed mean. Qualitative data shows significant improvements for 8/24 users, settle time 5-10 minutes, and strong music approval. YouTube views=6 represent critical discoverability gap despite positive user outcomes.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: This crisis protocol helps people in acute distress, but surprisingly few have discovered it despite those who do finding it very effective.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.7, confidence=meaningful. Analyzers consensus: standard crisis protocol performing as expected for its population. AutoGluon predicts ~7.96 suggesting potential alignment with model expectations. Qualitative data shows moderate improvements, settle time ~10 minutes, and mixed music opinions. YouTube views=4 indicate minimal reach but consistent with crisis protocol engagement patterns.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: A standard crisis protocol that performs as expected for users in distress, offering moderate relief for most.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful. Analyzers consensus: crisis protocol showing drifting analytical interpretation over time despite stable user reports. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, immediate settle for subset, and mixed music feedback. YouTube views=5 reflect ongoing discoverability challenges. Significant analytical drift (similarity=0.65) noted across multiple evaluations.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: A crisis protocol that helps many users, though analytical understanding of its effects has shifted somewhat over time.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong. Analyzers consensus: effective sleep protocol with fast onset of action. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, quick settle (5 min or immediate for 8/15), and strong music approval. YouTube views=4 indicate minimal discoverability. Performance aligns with HEALING sleep protocol expectations.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Excellent for sleep - users fall asleep quickly and consistently report liking how it sounds.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful. Analysts note: solid sleep protocol with minor user reports of rhythmic complexity. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, settle time 10-20 minutes, but subset described rhythm as 'too busy'. YouTube views=5 reflect low reach. Standard deviation lower than adjacent protocols suggesting consistent user experience.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Good for sleep that most users find helpful, though a few note the rhythm feels a bit busy for relaxation.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: standard sleep protocol performing within expected range. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, settle time 5-10 minutes, and balanced music/rhythm feedback. YouTube views=3 indicate minimal discoverability. No significant deviations from HEALING sleep protocol norms.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Standard sleep protocol that helps users relax and fall asleep, with consistent positive feedback.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: focus protocol performing below THRIVING series average. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, settle time ~5 minutes or immediate, but subset noted rhythm as 'slightly busy'. YouTube views=5 indicate low reach. AutoGluon implications suggest potential for refinement to reach series typical performance.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus protocol that helps some users but performs below average for this category, suggesting room for improvement.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.6, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: focus protocol delivering strong performance at series high end. Qualitative data shows moderate to significant improvements, settle time ~10 minutes, and strong music approval. YouTube views=4 reflect limited reach despite positive user feedback. Performance aligns with THRIVING focus protocol expectations.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Strong focus protocol that delivers clear benefits for most users, with good music approval and noticeable improvements.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: highest-performing focus protocol with strong outcomes and reach, though AutoGluon flags cross-bucket routing. Qualitative data shows significant improvements (11/29), settle time ~10 minutes, and excellent music approval. YouTube views=15 represent highest THRIVING reach. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (focus zone) with cross-bucket suggestions to X-ME-L1/X-MD-L1 indicating potential domain misalignment.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Top-performing focus protocol that many users find significantly helpful for concentration, with excellent discoverability relative to peers.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: creative flow protocol showing potential for greater efficacy than current observations suggest. AutoGluon implications (per gemma) predict higher outcome than observed mean. Qualitative data shows slight improvements most common, settle time varied, and generally positive music feedback. YouTube views=2 indicate extremely limited reach.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Shows promise for boosting creativity and flow, though current results suggest it could be even more effective than observed.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: creative flow protocol with moderate but reliable effects. Qualitative data shows significant improvements for 5/15 users, settle time 5-10 minutes, and balanced rhythm perceptions. YouTube views=2 reflect minimal discoverability. Performance consistent with T-CF-L1 but slightly higher mean and lower variance.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Delivers moderate but reliable creative flow benefits for users who engage with it fully.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing. Analysts consensus: promising creative flow protocol needing more data for full confidence. Qualitative data shows noticeable to significant improvements (13/25), settle time 5-10 minutes, and strong music approval. YouTube views=7 represent highest reach in this protocol subgroup. Lower variance suggests consistent user experience despite developing confidence tier.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Promising creativity booster that many users find significantly helpful, with strong music approval and decent discovery.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: reliable maintenance protocol with longer settlement times. Qualitative data shows moderate improvements, settle time often 20 minutes, and balanced music/rhythm feedback. YouTube views=3 indicate minimal discoverability. Performance consistent with THRIVING maintenance protocol expectations.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Reliable maintenance protocol that helps users feel balanced, though it takes longer (around 20 minutes) to settle in.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: maintenance protocol showing response variability that may reflect user subgroup differences. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, settle time 5-10 minutes, but subset reported rhythm as 'slightly busy'. YouTube views=4 reflect limited reach. Higher standard deviation suggests heterogeneous user experience requiring further investigation.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Maintenance protocol with mixed results - some users find it very helpful while others report more variable effects.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing. Analysts consensus: maintenance protocol where observed performance lags model predictions and analytical interpretation has shifted over time. AutoGluon predicts 7.69 (maintenance zone). Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements (6/19), but 4/19 reported 'did NOT settle'. YouTube views=3 reflect low reach. Significant analytical drift (similarity=0.65) noted across evaluations.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Maintenance protocol that helps many users feel balanced, but analytical understanding of its effects has changed over time and some report it doesn't settle them fully.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: mood/energy protocol where model predicts exceptional efficacy but current observations are modest. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 (low zone), highest of all protocols. Qualitative data shows moderate to significant improvements, settle time 10-20 minutes, and mixed music approval. YouTube views=8 reflect limited reach. Suggests investigating unidentified user subgroups or timing issues affecting observed outcomes.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Shows strong potential for boosting mood and energy according to predictive models, though current user results are more modest - suggesting either timing issues or unidentified user groups.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: mood/energy protocol performing as expected for its design parameters. Qualitative data shows significant improvements, settle time 10 minutes or immediate, and strong music approval. YouTube views=8 reflect limited but consistent reach. Performance aligns with TRANSFORMING mood/energy protocol expectations.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Reliable mood and energy booster that delivers consistent benefits for most users who engage with it fully.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Analysts consensus: mood/energy protocol showing divided responses - clear benefit for subset but minimal effect for others. Qualitative data shows noticeable to significant improvements (15/25), but 5/25 reported no effect. YouTube views=4 indicate minimal discoverability. Suggests user stratification to identify responsive subgroups versus non-responders.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mood booster that helps many users feel better but shows little effect for others, indicating it may work well for specific subgroups but not universally.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: motivation protocol with modest effects and potential retention issues indicating premature disengagement. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, settle time ~5 minutes, and balanced rhythm perceptions. YouTube views=5 indicate low reach. Suggests investigating whether current duration supports full entrainment before user drop-off.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Moderately helpful for motivation that works for some users, but many disengage early suggesting the current length may be too short for full effect.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing. Analysts consensus: developing motivation protocol performing below series peers. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements, settle time 5-10 minutes, and balanced rhythm perceptions. YouTube views=7 reflect limited reach. Performance consistent with early-stage TRANSFORMING motivation protocol development.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Developing motivation protocol that provides mild benefits for users who complete it, but performs below related protocols.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. Analysts consensus: motivation protocol with polarized responses - strong effects for some, minimal for others. Qualitative data shows moderate improvements, settle time 5-10 minutes, and balanced rhythm feedback. YouTube views=7 indicate limited reach. High standard deviation suggests heterogeneous user experience requiring subgroup analysis.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Motivation protocol with polarized effects - some users feel significantly more motivated while others notice little change, suggesting different user types respond differently.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: VOL001: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: standard mind clarity protocol performing within expected parameters. Qualitative data shows noticeable improvements for most, but 2/17 reported negative effects. YouTube views=8 reflect minimal reach. Performance aligns with TRANSFORMING mind clarity protocol expectations despite mixed qualitative feedback.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Standard mind clarity protocol that helps most users feel clearer, with a small subset reporting less positive experiences.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: VOL001: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing. Analysts consensus: developing mind clarity protocol where model predicts higher efficacy than current observations show. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 (mindclarity zone). Qualitative data shows significant improvements, settle time 10 minutes or immediate, and strong music approval. YouTube views=12 represent relatively higher reach in this subgroup.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Developing mind clarity protocol where users report good results, though models suggest it could be even more effective than current observations show.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: VOL001: n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45, confidence=developing. Analysts consensus: outlier protocol showing bimodal response patterns - effective for subset but problematic for others. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 (mindclarity zone), significant positive delta. Qualitative data shows mixed results with 2/17 reporting 'made things worse'. YouTube views=8 reflect limited reach. High variance suggests need for user stratification or redesign.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed results protocol - helps some users achieve mental clarity but seems to make others feel worse, suggesting it may not work for everyone in its current form.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset reveals HEALING and THRIVING maintaining strong, consistent performance while TRANSFORMING shows greater variability as expected from its design.
• HEALING: Highest response volume (n=574) with consistent outcomes across protocols (mean=6.98, std=1.77).
• THRIVING: Strong performance maintained (n=245, mean=6.96), particularly in Focus & Clarity protocols.
• TRANSFORMING: Lower mean scores (6.59) with highest variance (std=1.87), reflecting its ascending-arc design for low-affect states.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each service line demonstrates performance patterns aligned with its intended therapeutic mechanism.
• HEALING: Descend protocols show reliable nervous system regulation with lowest response variance.
• THRIVING: Hold protocols deliver consistent cognitive/emotional optimization, with strongest effects in focus applications.
• TRANSFORMING: Ascending-arc protocols exhibit expected variability in moving low-affect states toward regulation.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols show performance patterns that deviate from series expectations and warrant targeted investigation.
• X-MC-L3: Exceptionally low mean score (5.7) with high variability (std=2.45) suggests potential subgroup mismatch.
• T-MB-L3: Significant analytical drift (similarity=0.65) indicates evolving response patterns over time.
• H-CR-L1: Strong efficacy signals (n=43, mean=6.35) contrasted with minimal YouTube reach (6 views) implies discovery barriers.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements could enhance protocol effectiveness for specific user needs and contexts.
• X-MC-L3: Investigate response bifurcation and consider creating distinct variants for differing user profiles.
• T-MB-L3: Increase protocol duration by 20-30% to improve entrainment stability and reduce analytical drift.
• H-CR-L1: Enhance discovery pathways through adjusted metadata and cross-platform promotion.
• H-OS-L3: Extend hold phase duration to address observed settlement difficulties in subset of users.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding dynamics reveal both analytical evolution and meaningful protocol relationships.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 26 protocols show similarity <0.85, indicating evolving analytical frameworks as data accumulates.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: H-CR-L1 and H-CR-L2 form the tightest pair (sim=0.807), while X-MC-L3 remains analytically isolated (mean_sim=0.601).
• Sufficient run history exists for drift analysis, though recent increases in data volume may accelerate observed shifts.

by_protocol.X-ME-L2: VOL001: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Analysts consensus: mood/energy protocol performing as expected for its design parameters. Qualitative data shows significant improvements, settle time 10 minutes or immediate, and strong music approval. YouTube views=8 reflect limited but consistent reach. Performance aligns with TRANSFORMING mood/energy protocol expectations.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: VOL001: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Analysts consensus: mood/energy protocol showing divided responses - clear benefit for subset but minimal effect for others. Qualitative data shows noticeable to significant improvements (15/25), but 5/25 reported no effect. YouTube views=4 indicate minimal discoverability. Suggests user stratification to identify responsive subgroups versus non-responders.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: This anxiety protocol works well for most users, helping them feel significantly calmer. People really like the music and find it settles them in about 10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: A reliable choice for anxiety relief that delivers consistent, moderate improvements for most users who try it.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Standard anxiety protocol that helps users feel better, with most noticing improvements after about 10 minutes of listening.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: This crisis protocol helps people in acute distress, but surprisingly few have discovered it despite those who do finding it very effective.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: A standard crisis protocol that performs as expected for users in distress, offering moderate relief for most.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: A crisis protocol that helps many users, though analytical understanding of its effects has shifted somewhat over time.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Top choice for calming overwhelm - users consistently report feeling better and really liking the music, with decent discovery compared to other protocols.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Reliable option for reducing overwhelm that works well for most users who find it.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Generally helpful for overwhelm, though some users report it doesn't fully settle them even after the full duration.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Excellent for sleep - users fall asleep quickly and consistently report liking how it sounds.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Good for sleep that most users find helpful, though a few note the rhythm feels a bit busy for relaxation.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Standard sleep protocol that helps users relax and fall asleep, with consistent positive feedback.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Shows promise for boosting creativity and flow, though current results suggest it could be even more effective than observed.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Delivers moderate but reliable creative flow benefits for users who engage with it fully.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Promising creativity booster that many users find significantly helpful, with strong music approval and decent discovery.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus protocol that helps some users but performs below average for this category, suggesting room for improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Strong focus protocol that delivers clear benefits for most users, with good music approval and noticeable improvements.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Top-performing focus protocol that many users find significantly helpful for concentration, with excellent discoverability relative to peers.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Reliable maintenance protocol that helps users feel balanced, though it takes longer (around 20 minutes) to settle in.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Maintenance protocol with mixed results - some users find it very helpful while others report more variable effects.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Maintenance protocol that helps many users feel balanced, but analytical understanding of its effects has changed over time and some report it doesn't settle them fully.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Standard mind clarity protocol that helps most users feel clearer, with a small subset reporting less positive experiences.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Developing mind clarity protocol where users report good results, though models suggest it could be even more effective than current observations show.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Mixed results protocol - helps some users achieve mental clarity but seems to make others feel worse, suggesting it may not work for everyone in its current form.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Moderately helpful for motivation that works for some users, but many disengage early suggesting the current length may be too short for full effect.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Developing motivation protocol that provides mild benefits for users who complete it, but performs below related protocols.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Motivation protocol with polarized effects - some users feel significantly more motivated while others notice little change, suggesting different user types respond differently.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Shows strong potential for boosting mood and energy according to predictive models, though current user results are more modest - suggesting either timing issues or unidentified user groups.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Reliable mood and energy booster that delivers consistent benefits for most users who engage with it fully.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Mood booster that helps many users feel better but shows little effect for others, indicating it may work well for specific subgroups but not universally.
